zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan de Willoughby於1345年出生在英國林可爾史爾斯皮爾斯比艾雷斯比時，她的父親John Willoughby III Lord Willoughby17歲，母親Lady Cecily de Ufford18歲。她於1361年與Sir William de Astley 4th Baron Astley在英國林可爾史爾結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1390年在英國瓦維其可爾努聶亞頓享年45歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭諾圖波藍德莫普斯紐明斯特 艾比。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Joan de Willoughby于1345年出生在英国林可爾史爾斯皮爾斯比艾雷斯比时，她的父亲John Willoughby III Lord Willoughby17岁，母亲Lady Cecily de Ufford18岁。她于1361年与Sir William de Astley 4th Baron Astley在英国林可爾史爾结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1390年在英国瓦維其可爾努聶亞頓享年45岁，埋葬在英国英格兰諾圖波藍德莫普斯紐明斯特 艾比。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Willoughby
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of various places called Willoughby, for example in Leicestershire, Lincolnshire, Nottinghamshire, and Warwickshire. They are named from an Old English +wilig ‘willow’ + Old Norse bȳ ‘farmstead, village’.

variants.name: Wilby
meanings: habitational name from any of the places called Wilby, in Suffolk, Norfolk, and Northamptonshire. The first placename is probably named from an Old English wilig ‘willow’ + Old English bēag ‘circle, ring’; the Norfolk placename derives from Old English wilig + bēag or Old Norse bý ‘farmstead, village’; the Northamptonshire placename derives from the Old Norse personal name Vili + bý.
Redmonds has demonstrated that in the parish of Hipperholme, and subsequently elsewhere in Yorkshire, Wilby occurs as a variant of Willoughby .

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Joan de Willoughby was born in 1345, in Eresby, Spilsby, Lincolnshire, England, her father, John Willoughby III Lord Willoughby, was 17 and her mother, Lady Cecily de Ufford, was 18. She married Sir William de Astley 4th Baron Astley in 1361, in Lincolnshire, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. She died in 1390, in Nuneaton, Warwickshire, England, at the age of 45, and was buried in Newminster Abbey, Morpeth, Northumberland, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Joan de Willoughby est née en 1345, à Eresby, Spilsby, Lincolnshire, Angleterre, son père, John Willoughby III Lord Willoughby, avait 17 ans et sa mère, Lady Cecily de Ufford, avait 18 ans. Elle a épousé Sir William de Astley 4th Baron Astley en 1361, à Lincolnshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée en 1390, à Nuneaton, Warwickshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 45 ans, et a été enterrée à Newminster Abbey, Morpeth, Northumberland, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Joan de Willoughby 1345 in Eresby, Spilsby, Lincolnshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater John Willoughby III Lord Willoughby 17 und ihre Mutter Lady Cecily de Ufford 18. Sie hat 1361 in Lincolnshire, England Sir William de Astley 4th Baron Astley geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter. Sie starb 1390 im Alter von 45 Jahren in Nuneaton, Warwickshire, England und wurde in Newminster Abbey, Morpeth, Northumberland, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Joan de Willoughby è nata il nel 1345 nel Eresby, Spilsby, Lincolnshire, England, sua padre, John Willoughby III Lord Willoughby, aveva 17 anni e sua madre, Lady Cecily de Ufford, aveva 18 anni. Lei sposato Sir William de Astley 4th Baron Astley nel 1361, nel Lincolnshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 1 figlia. ja.lifeSummary: Joan de Willoughbyが1345年にイギリスリンコルンシュアイアスパルスビーエレスビーで生まれた時，父John Willoughby III Lord Willoughbyは17歳，母Lady Cecily de Uffordは18歳であった。1361年にイギリスリンコルンシュアイアでSir William de Astley 4th Baron Astleyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1390年にイギリスワーウィクシュアイアヌネアトンで45歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスノースウンバーランドモーペスニューミンスター アビーにて埋葬された。

ko.Newminster Abbey, Morpeth, Northumberlan: 영국 잉글랜드 노르스벌랜드 모르페스 뉴민스터 애브브이
ko.lifeSummary: Joan de Willoughby(이)가 1345년 영국 링코실르 스피스비 레스비에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John Willoughby III Lord Willoughby(은)는 17세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Lady Cecily de Ufford(은)는 18세였다. 그녀는 1361년 영국 링코실르에서 Sir William de Astley 4th Baron Astley(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1390년 영국 워윅실르 누니앳에서 45세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 노르스벌랜드 모르페스 뉴민스터 애브브이에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Joan de Willoughby nasceu em 1345, em Eresby, Spilsby, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, John Willoughby III Lord Willoughby, tinha 17 anos e sua mãe, Lady Cecily de Ufford, tinha 18 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir William de Astley 4th Baron Astley em 1361, em Lincolnshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 1 filha. Ela faleceu em 1390, em Nuneaton, Warwickshire, Inglaterra, com 45 anos, e foi sepultada em Newminster Abbey, Morpeth, Northumberland, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.Newminster Abbey, Morpeth, Northumberlan: нюминстир эбби, морпет, нортумбирланд, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joan de Willoughby родилась в 1345 г. в местности эресби, спилсби, линколншир, Англия, её отцу по имени John Willoughby III Lord Willoughby было 17 лет, а матери, которую звали Lady Cecily de Ufford, 18. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir William de Astley 4th Baron Astley в 1361 г. в местности линколншир, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь. Она умерла в 1390 г. в местности нунитон, варвиккшир, Англия в возрасте 45 лет и была похоронена в местности нюминстир эбби, морпет, нортумбирланд, Англия, Великобритания.

Se casó con Sir William de Astley 4th Baron Astley en 1361, en Lincolnshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 1 hija. Murió en 1390, en Nuneaton, Warwickshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 45 años, y fue sepultada en Newminster Abbey, Morpeth, Northumberland, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
